Tests switch statement behavior with labeled break in JavaScript.

# Purpose
This code demonstrates the use of a labeled `switch` statement in JavaScript. The label `switchMark` is applied to the `switch` statement, allowing the `break` statement in `case 1` to exit the labeled block. The `switch` statement evaluates the expression `1`, and since `case 1` matches, it executes the `break switchMark;` statement, which exits the `switch` block. The `case 2` contains an `assert(false);` statement, which is not executed because the `switch` statement exits before reaching it. This code provides a narrow functionality, illustrating control flow using labeled statements and `switch` cases.
